hero.question: Will the DHS shutdown end after March 31, 2026?
hero.prob_take: Buyers are pricing in Republican willingness to capitulate before a March 31 deadline, likely betting that shutdown theater loses its leverage once you hit the final week and economic pain becomes real. The market's underpricing the ugliness of a pre-election shutdown and the fact that both parties will fold rather than own the damage, making 68.8% too conservative for a "ends after March 31" outcome.

daily_take.headline: DHS Shutdown Odds Jump 10 Points. The Market Just Priced In Capitulation.
daily_take.deck: Congress folded on DHS funding overnight, sending "shutdown ends after March 31" from 58.7% to 68.8% in 24 hours. The sharp move signals real money believes the political will for a prolonged fight has evaporated. At 68.8%, you're betting on a quick resolution by end of Q1. That's reasonable if you trust the momentum, but 10-point moves on political theater usually mean the crowd overshot. Wait for a pullback to 65% before piling in.
